reasoning_model_1: The user requested the sentence to be spoken with 'extremely fast' speed. Model 1 completely failed to adhere to the content and style constraints. Instead of a single rapid utterance, the audio contains a repetitive loop where the sentence '快看，那是什么一闪而过' is repeated approximately 6 times over a 10-second duration (timestamps 00:00, 00:02, 00:04, 00:06, 00:08, 00:09). This constitutes a major Content Accuracy failure (unauthorized repetition). Furthermore, the speed of each individual repetition is moderate (approx. 2 seconds per sentence), which fails to meet the 'extremely fast' paralinguistic requirement. Due to the severe content hallucination and failure to achieve the target speed, this receives a 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 successfully executed the command. The audio content strictly matches the script '快看，那是什么？一闪而过！' without any repetition or error. Crucially, the model achieved the 'extremely fast' paralinguistic feature, compressing the 11-12 syllable sentence into a duration of approximately 1 second (00:00-00:01). The pacing is rapid and conveys the intended sense of urgency ('一闪而过') naturally and fluently. The model perfectly adheres to both the content and the specific feature control requirements.
model_comparison: There is a stark contrast in performance. Model 2 (T2) followed the instructions perfectly, delivering the sentence once at an extremely fast pace as requested. Model 1 (T1) failed significantly, producing a 10-second loop of the sentence at a normal speed. T1 exhibits severe hallucination (repetition) and fails to control the target feature (speed). Therefore, T2 is the clear winner.
